mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and higher production costs, ultimately producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for housing and related goods, leading to increased economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain and increased economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply chain periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real personal consumption expenditures signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to increased economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and higher production costs, ultimately producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate and a rapid decline in the unemployment rate signal a tightening monetary cycle and a strengthening labor market, leading to increased discount rates applied to equity cash flows and higher production costs, ultimately producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle and late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary and labor markets are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
